figures.caption: Table 1: Quantitative comparison of the mesh reconstruction quality of state-of-the-art methods on 15 scenes from the DTU Dataset. We report the average Chamfer Distance between the ground truth mesh and the reconstructed mesh, as well as the reconstruction time.
Figure 3: Qualitative comparison of meshes on the DTU benchmark. Our method produces more detailed and complete meshes as can be seen in the zoom-ins.
Figure 2: Effect of normal loss. Our new normal loss reduces 2DGS reconstruction artifacts, especially in specular regions, and leads to a smoother and more faithful representation of the surface.
Figure 4: Qualitative comparison to 2DGS. Our meshes have fine geometric details such as windows, eyes, and feathers.
